title: Change Linux Root Password
summary: This workflow provides two methods to change the root password in Linux: one for when the current password is known, and another for resetting it when the current password is unknown via the Grub menu.

root_steps.title: Method 1: Change password when current password is known

children.title: Open a terminal window
children.description: Press Ctrl+Alt+T to open a new terminal window or proceed if already at a command prompt.

children.title: Become root user
children.description: Type "su" and press Enter, then enter the current root password.

children.title: Exit root account and restart
children.description: Type "exit" and press Enter, then restart the computer.

root_steps.title: Method 2: Reset password via Grub menu when current password is unknown

children.title: Restart and access Grub menu
children.description: Restart the computer and press "E" at the Grub menu.

children.title: Modify Grub entry for single-user mode
children.description: Scroll to the line starting with "linux /boot", move cursor after "ro", and type "init=/bin/bash".

children.title: Boot to single-user mode
children.description: Press Ctrl+X to boot into a root-level command prompt.

children.title: Set new root password
children.description: Type "passwd" and press Enter, then enter the new password twice.
